orchestrator_notes: RT-4. Final todo 'Close project with 360 memory' can't be marked complete before doing it, but closeout validation (COMPLETION_BLOCKED) blocks if it's incomplete. Minor workflow friction. Feb report says 'documented workaround exists'. Likely close as by-design or document the workaround in the protocol.

sessions.title: Research: Todo Chicken-and-Egg
planned_tasks: Read complete_job() COMPLETION_BLOCKED logic â€” does it check ALL todos or just non-orchestrator todos?
Read the orchestrator protocol â€” does it tell orchestrators to add a 'closeout' todo item?
Check: what is the documented workaround? Is it 'mark todo as complete, then do closeout'?
Check: does the orchestrator mark its own closeout todo BEFORE or AFTER calling close_project?
Trace the FULL flow: orchestrator has todo 'Close project' -> tries complete_job -> COMPLETION_BLOCKED -> what next?
Determine: is this a real bug, a protocol gap, or just minor friction with a known workaround?
tasks_completed: Read complete_job() COMPLETION_BLOCKED logic (orchestration_service.py:1559-1807) - checks ALL todos, no exemptions
Read orchestrator protocol in thin_prompt_generator.py - continuation prompt line 147 documents correct sequence
Read both CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED implementations: project_closeout.py and write_360_memory.py
Verified workaround: mark todos completed via report_progress() BEFORE calling complete_job()
Traced full flow: write_360_memory -> report_progress(mark todos completed) -> complete_job -> close_project
Determined: NOT a real bug - standard 2-step sequence orchestrators handle naturally
Verified 0800b chain already added CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED recovery instructions to both prompt modes
deviations: No deviations. Research confirmed hypothesis: by-design behavior with documented workaround.

completion_blocked_mechanism.behavior: Queries ALL AgentTodoItem rows where status != completed. Raises ValidationError COMPLETION_BLOCKED. No exemptions.

closeout_todo_origin.created_by: Orchestrator agent via report_progress() - NOT system-created
closeout_todo_origin.explanation: Orchestrator creates its own todo list. Close project items are orchestrator-authored.

workaround_exists_and_is_documented.exact_text: proceed to closeout (update your own todos to completed via report_progress, then complete_job, then close_project_and_update_memory)
correct_sequence: 1. Do the closeout work (write 360 memory, drain messages)
2. Call report_progress() marking ALL todos as completed
3. Call complete_job() - succeeds because all todos completed
workaround_exists_and_is_documented.why_it_works: Orchestrator performs closeout ACTIONS first, marks TODO completed to record it, then calls complete_job(). Todo tracks completion, not permission.

reasoning: Chicken-and-egg framing based on misunderstanding. Orchestrator does NOT need complete_job() to perform closeout actions.
Correct flow: (1) Do the work, (2) Mark todo done, (3) complete_job(). Standard 2-step, not circular.
Continuation prompt documents this exact sequence at line 147.
write_360_memory() checks OTHER agents only (when entry_type=project_completion).
close_project_and_update_memory() checks OTHER agents, not calling orchestrator.
0800b chain already added CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED recovery to both CLI and multi-terminal prompts.

related_work.chain_0800: CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED recovery instructions added to orchestrator prompts in 0800b.

findings.recommendation: CLOSE RT-4 AS BY-DESIGN. No code changes needed. Workaround is the intended flow. 0800b strengthened orchestrator closeout guidance.

sessions.notes_for_next: 0806b session NOT NEEDED. RT-4 is by-design. Recommend cancelling 0806b.
sessions.summary: RT-4 is NOT a bug. complete_job() checks ALL todos, but intended flow is: (1) perform closeout actions, (2) mark todos completed via report_progress(), (3) call complete_job(). Documented in continuation prompt line 147. 0800b chain strengthened recovery instructions. Close as BY-DESIGN.

sessions.title: Implement: Todo Chicken-and-Egg Fix
sessions.notes_for_next: Cancelled. 0806a research determined no fix needed - RT-4 is by-design.
sessions.summary: Not needed. Research determined issue is by-design, not a bug.

chain_summary: RT-4 CLOSED AS BY-DESIGN. The todo chicken-and-egg is a misunderstanding of intended sequence. Orchestrator: (1) do work, (2) mark todos completed, (3) complete_job(). Documented in continuation prompt. 0800b added CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED recovery. No code changes. 0806b cancelled.
